heading: Assignment of members of Board
label: 7102 Assignment of members of Board
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 117–3001994— amended section generally, substituting present provisions for provisions authorizing Chairman to divide Board into sections of three members and to assign proceedings thereto, and provisions relating to assignment where section is composed of fewer than three members, limiting annual period of service, prohibiting more than one member to be a temporary or acting member, and relating to hearing docket and report of determination.Pub. First Training, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–300, § 1(b)136 Stat. 4379Dec. 27, 2022“The Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall ensure that each member of the Board of Veterans’ Appeals is offered the first annual training under , United States Code, as amended by subsection (a), by not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”section 7102(c) of title 38Dec. 27, 2022

Applicability of Limitation Under Subsection (c)(2), , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–300, § 1(c)136 Stat. 4379Dec. 27, 2022“The limitation under , United States Code, as added by subsection (a), shall apply with respect to the assignment of proceedings on or after the date that is 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”section 7102(c)(2) of title 38Dec. 27, 2022

children.label: a
children.text: A proceeding instituted before the Board may be assigned to an individual member of the Board or to a panel of not less than three members of the Board. A member or panel assigned a proceeding shall make a determination thereon, including any motion filed in connection therewith. The member or panel, as the case may be, shall make a report under on any such determination, which report shall constitute the final disposition of the proceeding by the member or panel.section 7104(d) of this title

children.label: b
children.text: A proceeding may not be assigned to the Chairman as an individual member. The Chairman may participate in a proceeding assigned to a panel or in a reconsideration assigned to a panel of members.

The Secretary shall ensure that there is offered to each member of the Board an annual training on military sexual trauma and proceedings that concern a claim for compensation based on military sexual trauma experienced by a veteran.

A proceeding that concerns a claim specified in paragraph (1) may not be assigned to an individual member of the Board or to a panel of members unless the individual member, or each member of the panel, as the case may be, has completed the annual training most recently offered to that member pursuant to such paragraph.
In this subsection, the term “military sexual trauma” has the meaning given that term in .section 1166(c) of this title
